claim_text: In nine surveyed municipal WWTP final effluents PFOA PFOS PFBS and PFHxS had 100% detection frequency and HFPO-DA had 67%; activated-sludge treatment was associated with increases of short-chain PFCAs consistent with precursor transformation
source_title: Occurrence Fate and Removal of PFAS in Small- and Large-Scale Municipal Wastewater Treatment Facilities in the United States
notes: Do not generalize detection frequencies to all plants

claim_text: Molecular simulation indicates that Arg122 substitution enlarges the hL-FABP outer pocket and disrupts PFOA hydrophobic packing and hydrogen bonding
source_title: Interaction of perfluoroalkyl acids with human liver fatty acid-binding protein
notes: Not a mutational binding experiment and does not prove a transferable dynamic material mechanism

claim_text: A cross-linked beta-cyclodextrin adsorbent removed PFAS and pharmaceuticals from municipal wastewater; affinity followed target hydrophobicity
source_title: Removal of PFAS and pharmaceuticals from municipal wastewater using a novel beta-cyclodextrin adsorbent over distinct contact times
notes: Shows a crowded high-performance host-guest baseline

claim_text: Chitosan-based molecularly imprinted polymers selectively adsorbed PFOS over structurally different anions
source_title: Selective removal of perfluorooctane sulfonate from aqueous solution using chitosan-based molecularly imprinted polymer adsorbents
notes: Demonstrates that static PFOS imprinting is not novel

claim_text: A Swiss Glatt Valley study reported 57-330 ng/L clarithromycin in treated WWTP effluent during summer
source_title: Occurrence and Fate of Macrolide Antibiotics in Wastewater Treatment Plants and in the Glatt Valley Watershed Switzerland
notes: Do not relabel the reported samples as secondary effluent without checking the process endpoint

claim_text: Macrolide antibiotics occupy defined sites in the bacterial large-ribosomal-subunit exit tunnel
source_title: The Structures of Four Macrolide Antibiotics Bound to the Large Ribosomal Subunit
notes: Supports static geometry; not sufficient for a material closing mechanism

claim_text: O-desmethylvenlafaxine showed low biodegradation and limited removal in a microalgae-based secondary-treatment system
source_title: Fate of priority pharmaceuticals and their main metabolites and transformation products in microalgae-based wastewater treatment systems
notes: Occurrence in the target local effluent remains to be measured

claim_text: SERT mediates Na+/Cl- dependent serotonin reuptake; the 2016 structures show an outward-open state and a central ligand site between TM1 3 6 8 and 10
source_title: X-ray structures and mechanism of the human serotonin transporter
notes: This paper alone does not resolve the complete alternating-access cycle and does not establish ODV-specific binding

claim_text: SMX SPY and their acetylated metabolites had the highest detection frequencies and concentrations in a 22-WWTP survey; the risk assessment identified SMX as HQ above 10 for algae
source_title: Ecotoxicity evaluation and removal of sulfonamides and their acetylated metabolites during conventional wastewater treatment
notes: Do not transfer the SMX ecological-risk result to AcSMX

claim_text: DHPS structures explain PABA/sulfonamide recognition and resistance mechanisms
source_title: Catalysis and Sulfa Drug Resistance in Dihydropteroate Synthase
notes: A reversible loop-closing material remains a design hypothesis

claim_text: ERR-gamma recognizes BPA through defined receptor interactions
source_title: Receptor binding characteristics of the endocrine disruptor bisphenol A for the human nuclear estrogen-related receptor gamma
notes: Does not independently establish a robust dynamic analogue

claim_text: In one study a carbamazepine molecularly imprinted polymer made with 4-vinylbenzoic acid reached 28.40 mg/g capacity and an imprinting factor of 2.72
source_title: Removal of the environmental pollutant carbamazepine using molecular imprinted adsorbents
notes: Evidence that conventional imprinting is crowded; not a class-wide capacity

claim_text: Nitrogen and phosphorus remain central targets for advanced treatment of wastewater secondary effluent
source_title: Simultaneous biological removal of nitrogen and phosphorus from secondary effluent of wastewater treatment plants by advanced treatment
notes: Background source

claim_text: A measured secondary effluent contained sulfate DOM nitrate and phosphorus at concentrations that create realistic competition
source_title: A comprehensive assessment on commercially available standard anion resins for tertiary treatment of municipal wastewater
notes: Use as a representative matrix not a universal composition

claim_text: Phosphate-bound C. perfringens PBP-1 is closed and completely buries phosphate between two domains using 14 hydrogen bonds from nine residues
source_title: Crystal structure of the phosphate-binding protein PBP-1 of an ABC-type phosphate transporter from Clostridium perfringens
notes: Use the exact count only for this protein

claim_text: PstS/PBP selectivity depends on protein and species; reports include at least roughly 500-fold discrimination against arsenate and up to about 10^5-scale discrimination against sulfate
source_title: Phosphate-binding proteins and peptides from molecular mechanisms to potential applications
notes: Do not present the factors as universal constants; pair quantitative claims with the relevant original study

claim_text: Nitrate-bound NrtA is a two-domain C-clamp that completely buries nitrate in the interdomain cleft; the authors propose that a C-terminal element may support transport-related conformational change
source_title: Atomic structure of a nitrate-binding protein crucial for photosynthetic productivity
notes: No paired apo/open and bound/closed structures; nitrate versus nitrite selectivity is limited

claim_text: In a model peptide covering the AfArsR metal-binding region As(III) induces a random-coil to ordered transition and forms a trigonal-pyramidal AsS3 site resembling the native protein
source_title: AsIII Selectively Induces a Disorder-to-Order Transition in the Metalloid Binding Region of the AfArsR Protein
notes: Not a whole-protein conformational experiment; municipal secondary-effluent relevance is conditional

claim_text: L-cysteine immobilized in electrospun membranes has already been presented as a bio-inspired arsenic adsorbent
source_title: Bio-inspired membranes for adsorption of arsenic via immobilized L-Cysteine in highly hydrophilic electrospun nanofibers
notes: Simple thiol immobilization is not a novel ArsR mimic

claim_text: Molecularly imprinted polymers are a mature route for pharmaceutical remediation but face real-water matrix challenges
source_title: Remediation of pharmaceuticals from contaminated water by molecularly imprinted polymers
notes: Used only to map prior-art density

claim_text: RCSB entry 1OIB is the Escherichia coli phosphate-binding protein T141D mutant and records a 321-aa protein entity
source_title: RCSB PDB 1OIB
notes: Database access verified 2026-07-16; not evidence that the project A197C construct has been expressed

claim_text: RCSB FASTA records for 1OIB and 1IXG are identical 321-aa mature chains
source_title: RCSB PDB FASTA 1OIB and 1IXG
notes: Source-chain SHA-256 78558e51049eec070b0f3249ffa8617b37a45b912436af1274ed746b57931a96; project mutations remain design definitions
